name: Coll de Femenia and Sa Batalla Loop
name_original: Collado de Feminia y Coll De Sa Batalla — circular desde Port de Pollença
description: Cycle the Collado de Feminia and Coll De Sa Batalla loop from Port de Pollença, a moderate 39.4-mile route with mountain views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Pollença is defined by its varied topography, ranging from challenging mountain passes to gentler coastal roads. The region is situated at the foot of the Serra de Tramuntana mountains, a UNESCO World Heritage site, offering significant elevation gains and winding roads. Coastal routes along the Bay of Pollença and towards Cap Formentor provide views of the Mediterranean Sea and dramatic cliffs. This landscape composition makes Pollença a destination for diverse road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Pollença The most popular road cycling route is Coll de Femenia and Sa Batalla Loop, a 39.4 miles (63.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 1 minute to complete. This route navigates through two notable mountain passes, Coll de Femenia and Sa Batalla. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Sa Bretxa Nova – Coll de Femenia (515 meters) loop from Lllenaire 1 (Port de Pollença), a moderate 44.3 miles (71.3 km) path. This loop starts from Port de Pollença and includes the climb to Coll de Femenia. Local road cyclists also love the Camí Vell de Pollença – Beautiful road loop from Gotmar 2 (Port de Pollença), a 31.7 miles (51.0 km) trail leading through the historic Camí Vell de Pollença, often completed in about 2 hours 7 minutes. Road cycling around Pollença is defined by the Serra de Tramuntana mountains, Cap Formentor, and coastal roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from demanding ascents to less strenuous coastal rides. The routes in Pollença are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 1,200 reviews. More than 48,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pollença's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pollença?
A: Pollença offers a wide variety of road cycling experiences, with over 60 routes available on komoot. These routes cater to different skill levels, from easy coastal rides to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What are the most challenging road cycling routes in the Pollença area?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the routes venturing into the Serra de Tramuntana mountains are ideal. A particularly demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32797104" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll dels Reis (682 m) – Sa Calobra Road loop from Gotmar 2 (Port de Pollença)&lt;/a&gt;, which includes the iconic Sa Calobra climb and features significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Pollença?
A: Yes, Pollença offers several less strenuous options. Routes along the coast, such as those around the Bay of Pollença, provide a beautiful and gentler cycling experience. While specific easy routes are not listed in the top routes, komoot features 23 easy road cycling tours in the area, perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride.

Q: What are the best times of year for road cycling in Pollença?
A: The mild Mediterranean climate makes spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) the best seasons for road cycling in Pollença. During these months, temperatures are pleasant, and there are fewer tourists, providing ideal conditions for exploring the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I expect along the road cycling routes?
A: Pollença's routes are rich with stunning vistas. The ride to Cap Formentor offers dramatic cliffside views and panoramic sights of the Tramuntana mountains and the bay, including the famous Es Colomer viewpoint. Routes to the Santuari de la Victoria also provide impressive views of Pollença and the surrounding bay after a climb.

Q: Are there any notable landmarks or attractions along the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Many routes pass by significant landmarks. The Serra de Tramuntana mountains themselves are a UNESCO World Heritage site. The Lluc Monastery is a popular destination within the mountains. For those interested in peaks, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1110992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Trail to Ermita de Nostra Senyora de Puig (Puig de Maria)&lt;/a&gt; offers historical interest and views. You can also find highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/189797" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talaia d'Alcúdia — Summit and Ruins&lt;/a&gt; nearby.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Pollença?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 1,200 reviews. Cyclists frequently praise the varied topography, from challenging mountain passes to beautiful coastal roads, and the breathtaking scenery, especially routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003228474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Femenia and Sa Batalla Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any loop routes for road cycling in Pollença?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Pollença are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003228474" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Coll de Femenia and Sa Batalla Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31423436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí Vell de Pollença – Beautiful road loop from Gotmar 2 (Port de Pollença)&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find routes that start from Port de Pollença?
A: Yes, Port de Pollença is a common starting point for many road cycling routes. For instance, the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33503513" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Bretxa Nova – Coll de Femenia (515 meters) loop&lt;/a&gt; begins from Lllenaire 1 in Port de Pollença, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31423436" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camí Vell de Pollença – Beautiful road loop&lt;/a&gt; also starts from Gotmar 2 in Port de Pollença.

Q: Are there options for road cycling with coffee stops or places to refuel?
A: Pollença and its surrounding towns are well-equipped for cyclists. Many routes pass through villages or towns where you can find cafes and restaurants to refuel. The cycling culture in Mallorca means that many establishments cater specifically to cyclists, offering convenient stops for coffee and snacks.

Q: What are the natural features that define road cycling in Pollença?
A: Road cycling in Pollença is characterized by the dramatic Serra de Tramuntana mountains, a UNESCO World Heritage site, offering challenging climbs and winding roads. The region also features stunning coastal roads along the Bay of Pollença and towards Cap Formentor, known for its dramatic cliffs and panoramic sea views. Natural reserves like S'Albufereta and La Gola provide additional scenic beauty.

Q: Is there public transport available for cyclists in Pollença?
A: Public transport options, including buses, are available in Pollença and connect to other towns in Mallorca. While some buses may accommodate bicycles, it's advisable to check the specific operator's policy and space availability, especially during peak seasons, as restrictions can apply.
tips: Relatively consistent gradient and nice views makes this climb a great gateway to the central area of la Serra de Tramuntana. Like many climbs in Mallorca, it turns out to be pretty exposed all the way up, so bear that in mind when you plan your route!
One of my favorites. The streets are in great conditions and the view is great. I enjoy going up and down just for training

The Santuari de Lluc is located at 525 meters altitude in a basin of the Tramuntana mountains. In addition to its sacred significance, it offers bikers above all a rewarding rest area with very good food. With its accommodations, it is also an idyllic starting point for bike tours in Mallorca. If you're looking for peace, you're right here.

Here you will find refreshments, tools and spare parts. And a great view of the surrounding area, including the Es Pla plain, Mallorca's granary. A short break is actually a must, even if the focus here is less on the idyll than on the cult factor.
Picture-perfect route - if e.g. the one in Selva starts by 8 a.m. with very little traffic
Great spot - most beautiful route from/to Caimari (to/from the NE)
Beautiful bike path, or 1.5 lane road that goes through the fields beautiful. Very good to drive in small groups. For large groups too narrow and too many potholes.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route in Port de Pollença?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Port de Pollença, you can typically find parking within the town. Look for public parking areas or check with your accommodation for cyclist-friendly options. There isn't a specific designated trailhead parking for this circular route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is milder, and the roads are less crowded than in peak summer. Summer can be very hot, making the climbs more challenging, though the pine trees offer some welcome shade.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees to cycle in the Serra de Tramuntana?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any specific permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route through the Serra de Tramuntana. The roads are public, and access is free for cyclists.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This route is a challenging road cycling loop, not suitable for dogs to accompany cyclists on the road, whether running alongside or carried on the bike. The distance, elevation, and traffic conditions make it unsafe and impractical for pets.

faqs.question: How challenging is the Collado de Feminia and Coll De Sa Batalla loop?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'moderate' and requires good fitness. Both the Ascent to Coll de Femenia from Pollença and the climb to Coll de Femenia (515 meters) feature consistent gradients, with some short, steep sections. Coll de Sa Batalla is considered an accessible climb with gentle slopes, though it has steeper parts towards the end. It's a classic Mallorca ride that offers a good test for road cyclists.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and road conditions can I expect?
faqs.answer: The route primarily follows the MA-10 road, which is known for its excellent tarmac. The surface is generally pristine, providing a smooth ride. However, be aware that there can be potential loose gravel on some corners, especially on descents. You'll encounter varied terrain, from challenging climbs with switchbacks to flatter recovery sections.

faqs.question: Are there any good places to stop for refreshments along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, a popular and highly recommended stop for cyclists is the Coll de Sa Bataia Café & Repsol Station at the summit of Coll de Sa Batalla. It's a great place to refuel, grab a coffee, and socialize with other cyclists before continuing your ride.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights or viewpoints along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll experience breathtaking scenery throughout the ride. Key highlights include the Coll de Femenia (515 meters), the historic Lluc Sanctuary, and the Coll de Sa Bataia Café & Repsol Station. You'll also enjoy panoramic views of the valley towards Pollença, the sea from Port de Pollença, and the distant Formentor peninsula on clear days, especially from viewpoints like View of Caimari and Selva.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the El Camí IT4TR3 El Camí Illenc: de l'Alguer a Formentera, Alcudia - Campanet, and [CIMA IB05] Puig de Tomir - Femenía. You'll also find it briefly touching parts of the GR 221 Dry Stone Route: Stage 8: Pont Romà - Port de Pollença and the GR 222 - Ruta Artà-Lluc.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: For this moderate road cycling route, you should bring your racebike in good condition, appropriate cycling attire, a helmet, plenty of water, energy snacks, a basic repair kit (spare tube, pump, tire levers), and sun protection. Don't forget your phone for navigation and emergencies, and some cash or a card for the café stop.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: While Coll de Sa Batalla is considered an 'accessible' climb, the overall route is rated as 'moderate' and requires good fitness due to its distance (over 63 km) and significant elevation gain (around 850m). It's not typically recommended for absolute beginners to road cycling, but those with some experience and a decent fitness level should find it manageable and rewarding.
